title: sterculia apetala var. apetala
intro: Sterculia apetala var. apetala is a plant species, also known as the "Indian horse radish," belonging to the family Malvaceae. Traditionally, it is used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve digestion. In terms of herbal preparations, the roots of Sterculia apetala var. apetala are often used to make a decoction or a powder, which can be consumed in various forms, such as tea or capsules, to reap its medicinal benefits.
